howItWorks.intro: This coast fire calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is Coast FIRE?
faq.answer: Coast FIRE means you have saved enough that compound growth alone will carry you to your retirement target — no more contributions needed. You still need to cover current expenses.

faq.question: How is Coast FIRE different from FIRE?
faq.answer: FIRE means you can stop working entirely. Coast FIRE means you can stop saving for retirement but still need income for living expenses. You can switch to a lower-paying, more fulfilling job.

faq.question: What assumptions matter most?
faq.answer: Return rate has the biggest impact. A 1% difference over 30 years changes the outcome dramatically. Use conservative estimates (6-7% nominal).

faq.question: Is this realistic?
faq.answer: Historically, a diversified stock portfolio has returned about 10% nominally. After inflation, about 7%. Coast FIRE works if you stay invested long-term and do not panic sell.
